source_refs.html: /Users/toy/English/躺着学/躺着学阅读/7371_Passage 3 Children’s literature studies today.html

passages.title: Children’s literature studies today
passages.source_html:
Children’s literature studies today


A 


Who studies children’s literature and what is it that they study? The answers to this question are complex and messy, because of the many confounding factors which exist in this field.


B


Firstly, unlike literature for adults. Children's literature is not generally written by its own readers. Adults write for children, and thus adult perceptions of what children are and of what they could and should be become woven into the literature.


C


Furthermore, some of those who study children’s literature (and those who write certain kinds of children's books) are less interested in literary values than in the kinds of lessons it can teach – either in terms of creating better children or in terms of serving a particular curriculum. The issue of how a teacher can use a children’s book is often contentious, but even outside the classroom, much material for children is still didactic.


D


Thirdly, while almost all literature is currently promoted within a strong commercial matrix, children’s literature is often especially targeted for marketing initiatives. This fact means that readers are often recruited with a message that is negligibly literary and significantly oriented to ideas of consumption. Daniel Hadetzx (2002) has raised useful questions about whether children’s experience of reading is altered when their books are part of a larger marketing framework involving the movie, the game, and the toy of a popular children’s book. How children perceive and respond to their stories in this new context is an important question.


E


It is also important to note that texts in an ever-increasing range of new media compete with print media for the attention of the child reader, and create definitional issues for scholars. Does the term literature’ exclusively imply a verbal text? If not, where are the limits? Could a literary computer game ever be considered a work of literature? If not, what kind of attention should be paid to it, since children themselves undoubtedly perceive their print literature as part of a broader continuum? The internet provides one forum through which children now communicate with each other. (In 2003, the internet search engine Google listed 7,920,000 sites relating to the Harry Potter novels; even allowing for duplication and dead ends, that is a number with revolutionary implications.)


F


Finally, in the context of the higher education institutions where the formal study of children’s literature of often located, at least three disciplinary frameworks (English, education, and librarianship) fragment the focus of scholarly study of children’s literature.


G


How is the value of the imaginative encounter with the work of literature sustained and honored among such a welter of conflicting interests? One route through this maze is to ask the child readers for help. As David Lewis (2001) has perceptively noted, what children think of reading is not usually the same as what adults think, whether teachers or parents. As Lewis points out children 'sometimes see more and they often see differently'. If those who study reading can explore children’s perceptions as well as those of adults, their understanding of the nature of reading will be enhanced.


H


Lewis makes a further valid point when he adds that exploring children’s perceptions is usually justified for educational reasons: “It is true that a better understanding of how children read and how they learn to read, is a prerequisite to improved approaches to teaching. However, it can also be argued, as Lewis rightly does, that when children’s responses to literature are accessed and interpreted, they frequently lead to an understanding of how picture books appeal to children.


I


Young people’s accounts of what and how they read also enable a more sophisticated description of many of the complex processes involved in reading. All descriptions of reading run the risk of solipsism: i.e. this is how I read so this is what reading is for everyone. Asking other readers how they read, however, reduces that risk. For example, if I am a strong visualizer as I read, I may consider that visualization is a key component of successful reading and I may judge books by their capacity to evoke a vivid visual response. Other readers, however, may help me to realize that not everyone reads with mental pictures. Some readers respond to the patterns of the words, 'hearing' them inaudibly like a subliminal radio program. Others respond to the patterns of feelings in the story, responding with an emotional connection. Talking to competent readers, of all ages, provides a better understanding of reading experiences.


J


Children’s insights are even more important when it comes to understanding the significance of print literature as one aspect of literary culture. Too often adults assume that reading any book at all is a more worthwhile experience than playing a digital game of any kind. A humbler approach would include asking why the game appeals to the player. Many adults will probably never develop the automatic skills to process a game as readily as they can read a book. This does not indicate that a book is better, but that a particular set of skills is absent. Non-players must acknowledge that some fictional universes are thus closed to them, and a logical response would be to find someone who can guide them to the pleasures and challenges of the gaming world. Games need to be judged individually just as books do, and any evaluative framework needs to take this into account.

groups.title: Questions 27-40
groups.source_html:
Questions 27 - 29


Choose the correct answer.

27

Which of the following best summarises the writer’s argument in the second paragraph?

正确答案： A. Children are portrayed as adults see them.

审题： 题目要求总结第二段的主要论点，关注成年人如何看待儿童文学以及这种观点如何影响儿童的表现。


定位关键词：in the second paragraph


对应原文：B段，“Adults write for children, and thus adult perceptions of what children are and of what they could and should be become woven into the literature.”


解题思路：B段指出了儿童文学通常由成年人创作，因此这些作品不可避免地包含了成人对儿童的看法和期望。这意味着儿童在文学作品中被呈现成成人所理解的样子，与“儿童被描绘成成人的视角”相符。相比之下，文中并未讨论儿童的创作能力，只是指出主要是成年人为儿童写作。原文没有指出成人是否成功或失败地激发了儿童的想象力。该段没有涉及成人文学的难度问题。


同义替换：portrayed=woven into the literature

28

In the third paragraph, what does the writer say is the main interest of some people who study children’s literature?

正确答案： C. the instructive nature of children’s books

审题：题目要求识别第三段中对某些儿童文学研究者主要兴趣的描述。


定位关键词：main interest, third paragraph


对应原文：C段，“Furthermore, some of those who study children’s literature (and those who write certain kinds of children's books) are less interested in literary values than in the kinds of lessons it can teach – either in terms of creating better children or in terms of serving a particular curriculum.”


解题思路：C段指出研究儿童文学的人有时更关注书籍的教育意义而非其文学价值。 “写作质量”不符合，因为原文没有强调这一点。原文关注的是教育性而非想象力，“儿童在故事中的描写方式”没有被提及。


同义替换：instructive nature=kinds of lessons it can teach

29

The main point of the writer’s argument in the fifth paragraph is to

正确答案： B. explore the impact of computers on the boundaries of children’s literature.

审题：题目询问第五段的主要论点，关注计算机对儿童文学界限的影响。


定位关键词：the fifth paragraph


对应原文：E段，“It is also important to note that texts in an ever-increasing range of new media compete with print media for the attention of the child reader, and create definitional issues for scholars.”


解题思路：E段指出新媒体（如计算机）如何与传统印刷媒体竞争，并讨论这对儿童文学界限的影响。学者认为电脑游戏是儿童文学的延伸，并未在原文中指出。文学和电脑游戏的起源不同”没有被强调。证明儿童使用电脑多于阅读文学”原文并未做出这样的比较。

Questions 30 - 34


Choose YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer, choose NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer, or choose NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

30

Children tend to make a clear distinction between print literature and electronic media. 

正确答案： B. NO

审题： 题目询问儿童是否区分印刷文学与电子媒体。


定位词： "distinction between print literature and electronic media"

对应原文：E段，“It is also important to note that texts in an ever-increasing range of new media compete with print media for the attention of the child reader, and create definitional issues for scholars. Does the term literature’ exclusively imply a verbal text? If not, where are the limits? Could a literary computer game ever be considered a work of literature? If not, what kind of attention should be paid to it, since children themselves undoubtedly perceive their print literature as part of a broader continuum?”


解题思路：E段指出儿童并不会将印刷文学与其他媒体明确地区分开来，而是将其视为一个更广泛、互相联系的整体。

31

The study of children’s literature at higher education institutions is restricted to one subject area.

正确答案： B. NO

审题： 题目询问儿童文学研究是否局限于一个学科。

定位词关键词： restricted to one subject area

对应原文：F段，“Finally, in the context of the higher education institutions where the formal study of children’s literature of often located, at least three disciplinary frameworks (English, education, and librarianship) fragment the focus of scholarly study of children’s literature.”


解题思路：F段指出儿童文学的研究涉及多个学科框架，明确表明该领域的研究并非局限于单一学科，选项中的说法与原文内容相矛盾。

32

Exploring children’s perceptions of reading will assist parents to choose suitable books for children.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

审题： 题目询问儿童阅读的感知对父母选书的帮助。


定位关键词："perceptions of reading", "parents", "suitable books"


对应段落： G段, “As David Lewis (2001) has perceptively noted, what children think of reading is not usually the same as what adults think, whether teachers or parents.”


解题思路： G段指出，理解儿童阅读的必要性，但并未指出这将直接帮助父母选择适合的书籍。

33

Adults may appreciate the appeal of illustrated stories better, if they have more information on how children read.

正确答案： A. YES

审题： 题目询问成人如何通过理解儿童的阅读方式来更好地欣赏图画书。

定位关键词： appreciate the appeal, better, more information


对应原文： H段， "However, it can also be argued, as Lewis rightly does, that when children’s responses to literature are accessed and interpreted, they frequently lead to an understanding of how picture books appeal to children."


解题思路：H段指出，理解儿童如何阅读可以帮助成人更好地欣赏图画书的吸引力。


同义替换：appreciate=understand

34

Children should be asked what features they would like digital games to include.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

审题： 题目询问是否应该询问儿童对数字游戏的看法。

定位词关键：features, digital games include


对应原文：J段，“A humbler approach would include asking why the game appeals to the player.”


解题思路：J段未提及儿童对于数字游戏应包含哪些特征的具体看法或建议，这一选项没有在文中明确表达出来。

Questions 35 - 39


Complete each sentence with the correct ending. Choose the correct ending and move it into the gap.

Options (Word Bank):
A. accepting that literature exists in a variety of forms today
B. verbalising the words in their heads
C. discovering the reading techniques used by others
D. the style of written stories changing over time
E. the lack of some specific abilities
F. a deeper knowledge of the intricacies of reading
G. children teaching adults to play computer games
H. creating a variety of images in their minds.
Question / ParagraphABCDEFGH
35

Young people’s accounts of how they read lead to ________
✅ 正确答案：F

审题： 题目询问年轻人对自己阅读方式的描述所带来的结果。

定位关键词： "Young people's accounts of how they read"

对应原文： I段， "Young people’s accounts of what and how they read also enable a more sophisticated description of many of the complex processes involved in reading."


解题思路：I段指出年轻人对其阅读过程的描述使得人们能够获得对阅读复杂性的更深入理解。


同义替换：deeper knowledge = more sophisticated description； intricacies of reading = complex processes involved in reading
36

The risk of solipsism is reduced by ________
✅ 正确答案：C

审题： 题目询问如何减少自我中心主义的风险。

定位关键词： "risk of solipsism"

对应原文： I段， "All descriptions of reading run the risk of solipsism: i.e. this is how I read so this is what reading is for everyone. Asking other readers how they read, however, reduces that risk."


解题思路： I段指出对阅读的所有描述都存在自我中心主义的风险：即‘这是我如何阅读的方式，所以这就是所有人阅读的方式’。然而，询问其他读者他们是如何阅读的，可以降低这种风险。
37

Strong visualisers judge books on the basis that they are________
✅ 正确答案：H

审题： 题目询问强视觉化的读者如何评判书籍。

定位词： "strong visualisers"

对应原文：I段， "For example, if I am a strong visualizer as I read, I may consider that visualization is a key component of successful reading and I may judge books by their capacity to evoke a vivid visual response.”


解题思路：I段指出强视觉化的读者会根据书籍是否能够激发他们的想象力来评判书籍的价值。


同义替换：creating a variety of images =evoke a vivid visual response
38

Children’s insights are important in ________
✅ 正确答案：A

审题： 题目询问儿童的见解在何种方面重要。

定位词： "Children's insights"

对应原文：J段， "Children’s insights are even more important when it comes to understanding the significance of print literature as one aspect of literary culture."


解题思路：J段指出儿童的见解对于接受和理解今天文学文化的多种形式（包括印刷文学）非常重要。
39

When adults read a book more easily than they play a digital game it simply suggests ________
✅ 正确答案：E

审题： 题目询问成年人在阅读书籍和玩数字游戏时的能力差异意味着什么。

定位关键词： "read a book more easily"


对应原文：J段， "This does not indicate that a book is better, but that a particular set of skills is absent."


解题思路： J段指出成年人在阅读书籍时的便利性并不代表书籍更优秀，而是缺乏某些特定的技能。

Questions 40


Choose the correct answer.

40

What was the writer’s main purpose in writing this article?

正确答案： C. to review the challenges in the field of children’s literature studies and suggest how to proceed

审题： 题目要求明确作者写这篇文章的主要目的。

定位词关键词： main purpose

对应原文： J段，" This does not indicate that a book is better, but that a particular set of skills is absent. Non-players must acknowledge that some fictional universes are thus closed to them, and a logical response would be to find someone who can guide them to the pleasures and challenges of the gaming world. Games need to be judged individually just as books do, and any evaluative framework needs to take this into account."


做题思路：段落A 指出儿童文学主要是由成年人创作，因此反映了成年人的视角，提出了儿童的声音往往被忽视。

段落B 指出一些研究者更关注儿童文学的教育功能，而非文学价值，进一步探讨了这一点的复杂性。

段落C 指出了市场营销对儿童阅读体验的影响，质疑了如何在商业化背景下理解儿童的阅读。

段落D 指出了电子媒体对儿童文学的定义和范围的影响，提出了对“文学”定义的挑战。

段落E 指出了儿童文学研究在高等教育中的学科分散问题，反映出研究的多样性。

段落F-H 引入David Lewis的观点，强调理解儿童阅读的必要性，认为儿童的反馈对教育和文学价值评估都至关重要。

段落I, J总结了以上讨论，强调在众多利益冲突中保持对儿童文学价值的理解是多么重要。

通过这些分析，作者不仅呈现了儿童文学研究的现状和挑战，还呼吁学者们更多地关注儿童的视角和反馈，探索如何在现代社会中促进儿童阅读，选项C最符合作者的目的：回顾儿童文学研究的挑战并提出未来的研究方向。
